task: Write a Python function count_distinct_over(nums, k) returning how many distinct values in the list are strictly greater than k, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly counts the number of distinct values in the list that are strictly greater than k.
trace: I used a Python generator expression to iterate through the numbers and store them as unique key-value pairs in a set. Since sets only allow unique elements, this effectively counts each distinct number. I then checked if each number is greater than `k`, filtering out any numbers less than or equal to `k`. The length of the resulting set will give us the count of distinct numbers that are strictly greater than `k`. To handle an empty list, the function returns 0.
